textByHash.d7bed44f85f45d70d0322076880a84bdabc70cae: Very well, I see you have a close relationship with your cat, I will help you if I can.
textByHash.6b663f25d5deb3888bb670de8955670e1c3edcba: Who is this Robert the Strong that you speak of?
textByHash.1ca08841c0c5306c175b089e137387adcfb71ce2: It is true that there is something about Bob... there is the mark of power on him, I have ignored it for too long.
textByHash.84af1dead55600f825efd7040b6d261bb5a3c99d: Then it is the time for action!
textByHash.6ef602c0b9bded85675b4a4f74f6043ffb4f01f3: Come with me into the desert so we are not disturbed.
textByHash.50c63457c94ae02cf87cb3449df77b40b351b349: Quiet please.... I shall summon Bob.
textByHash.142f34969efe1f33cfe05a872a79f6d5e2f0b030: I have brought you here to find out who you really are.
textByHash.8fba68e3fb3a0512a06d9883c82881ba63a0f22f: I have long suspected that your appearance as an ordinary cat belies your true nature.
textByHash.35278ada036c7b39e8a38781ffb4a11e242b13e6: You are now under my influence.
textByHash.ed55f9c687cf955d0601a51cba9446a6501e7d5b: Let's start with something simple. What is your name?
textByHash.ca3c2ca083666cb7ff4016cd9bbbc87810ee52d1: Good. Are you or have you ever been called Robert the Strong?
textByHash.e7fded900fee9fbeecde0e8db0175790514851da: Think back to your earliest memories.
textByHash.e7a0b478974ecc2a7d951f55a0acc887ed88a4da: You are no longer under my influence.
textByHash.30b8c44356a137569ad19c2544842575c715a6a4: There are more important matters than match making.
textByHash.0734f9d7cbb1e81b10468f71b3475f94b137f8b6: Very well! I shall summon Neite!
textByHash.23b67ba6910979b56a1b405ab43ad8dbab6328da: For too long I have ignored the fact that there is a mark of power on Bob. Under my influence Bob was able to recall memories that had remained hidden until now. It proves he is, or was, Robert the Strong.
textByHash.ab88e44713c884e495174a009f2fdd78c98bc196: , since you are to look after Unferth, shall I teleport you to his house?
textByHash.75d2be789f8c4c87f4973a3737889c09520a4e15: Have you completed the chores that Bob asked you to?
textByHash.65f2999a3e72a02d5d9328e896e065c5d5cf525f: Hello, human. Bob was just telling me about your dungeon.
textByHash.9b1a3e1b07ed54bacf11a06d28667b22471f00a5: Ah yes. That amulet of yours is very useful, but it has limitations. I can use my power to grant you the ability to talk to cats even when you're not wearing it.
textByHash.afee67f6cebe49efc1a4bcc88b5200fd461394d2: Very few humans are ever given this power, but you've proven yourself to be a friend to feline kind.
textByHash.52eec2b320e2fcba9d56b6b1159528aa4826c614: All done. Now, what is it you need my help with?
textByHash.31e2d388e96cdbe67e5e86f334fbbeb3e29cfbc1: What is it you wish to learn?
textByHash.98289c8ac6c723791d47bfa31a136d0d8436495e: I'm afraid I will be of little help here. All I can do is hypnotise Bob. I can't enter his mind and choose which memory to view.
textByHash.9558449242bf2a031ef57172bf78ce7e8b7aee9e: Ah, how interesting... a cat. Come here to me, kitty.
textByHash.1050b3e523a41421413e891124a00779eeb77e3e: Yes, your companion tells me that you are in a spot of bother.
textByHash.2bdc06d67bab959e375c22bd92e2f4f395ce32a4: Interesting, very interesting. I'm afraid I do not know what happened to you, but it sounds like quite a bit occurred between you meeting that wanderer and now.
textByHash.75d61998fd672eb90e3364466cbde485a0324e85: Then I suggest you find something to remind you. I find that important objects and locations can trigger memories, no matter how repressed they are.
textByHash.58081a4bc9cf7f718eff5a67ecd0dbde71233308: We've already had this conversation, have we not? You're in a spot of bother, but you can't remember what happened to you.
textByHash.2620cdcaec49d56a78a03d2912811c7de8085835: Perhaps you should try to find something to remind you again. I find that important objects and locations can trigger memories, no matter how repressed they are.
textByHash.7e6bf4704c9e33a240cbf494a7bc932f2c9ee1f9: I have no interest in speaking with you. You should bring your cat to me again. The two of us have much to discuss.
textByHash.34c01a86a547eb527f752fcf7710f99f72938930: Yes, your cat tells me you're in a spot of bother. Something to do with grave robbing, tisk tisk. So unbecoming of a guardian of cats.
textByHash.4b46875e4dba7b1d8e0608957423a16e71b3f4bb: Yes, your cat told me already. Something to do with grave robbing, tisk tisk. So unbecoming of a guardian of cats.
textByHash.f616e9f37009ad4c64c59c5a1933c0ab3bfd0db6: Well, I have to say I enjoy a laugh at someone's expense now and again... so let's have a bit of fun.
textByHash.d193477072fa8dcb53a30c933004344ee30e3251: I'll help you if you can solve a simple puzzle of mine, but...
textByHash.fbb86af9c4868bc32db4a715e20d49e1e0936f31: ... but if you answer incorrectly, I get to keep your cat.
textByHash.4489f7792c6897f28cd191acb560a55269eb9a90: A husband and wife have six sons and each son has one sister. How many people are in the family?
textByHash.920cbd9709c67c4eb0980a2bafabd640ff80f237: I think your master has proven that he is too foolish to look after you. Come with me.
textByHash.d1b09501d39eb5a72b010da2b5563080f23464a0: I think your master has proven that she is too foolish to look after you. Come with me.
textByHash.1aa02ebd3f768f03822f9797a368def8071f3b5e: Well, you should have thought of that before you risked it for some advice from a crazy old Sphinx.
textByHash.7807a9e832aaee702dcaf840b668a42a4d068531: What was it you needed help with? Oh yes, I remember now. You're a grave robber.
textByHash.b1482f776b698c708f920f99ef46b90d50971f08: How disappointing. Still, nothing ventured, nothing gained. Goodbye.
textByHash.750d9d081c1e82917b625b456df165b8484c1770: Well answered, human. I guess you get to keep your cat.
textByHash.7566671f58a3edb6cc116ca4223d894fe5adfeb8: Too true, you didn't rob just anybody's grave. You managed to rob the grave of Klenter, the recently deceased High Priest of Icthlarin. Do you know what that means?
textByHash.4b93f145f4110268d0d15de1b716ee7ab7c45bea: Icthlarin is the Menaphite god of the dead. By desecrating the tomb of one of his priests, you've made an enemy out of every person in this city.
textByHash.25d3d56abb0a2ad12857d7a1d2358848c0a9f255: Really? Because if I'm not mistaken, that's Klenter's shade right over there. He definitely doesn't seem happy with you. There's also the burial jar in your possession. How do you explain that?
textByHash.2c2504a2f43017141352bd0090f10944614a822f: I can smell the rotting organs from here.
textByHash.24644af96081b46d2e11dc52b68584e9972712dd: Interesting. I'm afraid I can't help you. I have my own concerns without involving myself with petty human affairs.
textByHash.447e8f8e409e7c34e4282bf35856dce9e7c01cea: However, I suggest you seek out the new High Priest of Icthlarin to the south east. He might be able to assist you. Of course, you'll need to convince him to talk to you first.
textByHash.67a182d4d12bb1d70cbf79aabb74df5024bec794: Here, I have a token that he will know is from me. Take that to him, and I'm sure he will speak with you.
textByHash.214f11af2f810835649091e16c634ec753a6e0d5: Well, you would be well advised to get another.
textByHash.a75030a7ff384e0561c357a4d1e0da2e353ca2ea: I am an agent of neither good nor evil. I am unconcerned with the plight of mankind and their petty wars and beliefs.
textByHash.57561b36fe38777592db7f3c2a6cfecbc9130e8d: I am the guardian of feline kind.
textByHash.6fcfbe68ae4f66ffb7c26c55e3db2ba4c96d616c: Cats play a significant role in the religion of this area, particularly when it comes to guarding the dead from the Devourer.
textByHash.214d1fb6b6b2839c7438bc0d99bc860097108be8: She's the very incarnation of destruction.
textByHash.ed7099ff909576077e3b527c70aec4dbbb3ce23c: What understanding do you have of the Menaphite gods?
textByHash.5510f6615a4a64bbe840a09cb8430f6a0b234326: Well the Menaphite Pantheon is made up of four main deities.
textByHash.7dae798a326065de61d1509b763b7b4054f74571: The two main gods of the pantheon are Tumeken, the god of light, and Elidinis, the goddess of fertility.
textByHash.ff1d4c24adbfa5a50d9b52759ba8e09e9eecc2b7: Tumeken and Elidinis also have two children. Icthlarin is one, and the Devourer is the other.
textByHash.58e84b8edd62d715c3a0d212bd9659aabb8823f0: She was not always called such. She is known by her deeds now, rather than her name.
textByHash.1009a952b2506f841a4dc763dfe430a40bb039e2: That is the common belief, but things are rarely that simple. In some ways, neither of them are good or bad.
textByHash.665ca9f8f13f9578b2dcffd3e56309909827329d: Icthlarin is the god of the dead. He takes care of the passing of souls to their next life. The Devourer is the goddess of destruction, and that is what she craves.
textByHash.e1a8f64907e6d25397eeebb62b9974cd700a8cdb: That's not quite what I said. Still, is destruction enough to make one evil? Fire destroys, but is that evil?
textByHash.60c2726d3aea6c10858ef0d961180acb6ccb3cd7: We could have an overlong theological debate now, but let's instead get to my point. Destruction has a place, so maybe the Devourer does as well. However, she has taken it too far.
textByHash.af6321116ee1e695ca51bb171e3c19a6c7d443a3: The Devourer wishes to destroy every soul in existence, living or dead. As you can imagine, this has brought her into conflict with Icthlarin.
textByHash.5869004fbe39d58365f603cfab0487ecffeafe7c: Well anticipated. The Devourer is terrified of cats for reasons I will not explain. However, her followers, although few in number, are not. I ensure the survival of cats. For this service, Icthlarin grants me eternal life.
textByHash.cb2e132d6a0be9d5b81c5133104ba8ae29ada22c: Nothing of the sort. As I've said before, I have no interest in humans and their gods. I look after my kind. For that, Icthlarin looks after me.
textByHash.0377ae9a11ddd18ea77e5401327ba88179c51721: I thought that would be pretty obvious.
textByHash.d65d780b158865cb7360983a5b9ae345bb7fcdc0: Flattery, my adventurer, will get you everywhere. But to answer your question, Sophanem has been struck with plagues.
textByHash.c56018ff75b737f14d0e6bedbd41ba8495f98c4e: Quite a number. There's a plague of frogs to the west. In the east, you can also find locusts.
textByHash.947a8a1ed287ecfb6c4390465296bca4050826c6: The cows have only been producing sour milk since the outbreak, and the residents have been struck down by a rash of spots.
textByHash.0b2702bd459a145627342b129b713ee25a1559b8: Sophanem is called the city of the dead because the dead are brought here. Death plays a rather large role in the beliefs of the people and, as a result, the business.
textByHash.0ef3cb189451df395d638c7a65644b85882fe8c7: Ah, human. How go things, great guardian of cats?
textByHash.1d1044e1191d27e1d0746c52362d18a4de51ea44: I don't need to mock you, human. You're doing a good enough job of that on your own.
textByHash.bc2c39cd1718925270d944932dc8c4902937ddd8: Do you think that you'll be able to communicate with me at a deeper level because you're wearing that amulet of catspeak?
textByHash.cd22a820ceaa9b0950e048c4ea2961e01da7b59a: So, what do you need human?
textByHash.38e64c1410e4e3cd95f364b50fc145f8fbb4436b: If you wish to know about the lesser gods, you should speak with Jex at the Temple of the Lesser Gods. I suppose I could spare a moment or two to tell you about the main four though.
textByHash.3931cbfe0d94a48321fd4325772838674e90bd01: Tumeken is the god of light and husband to Elidinis. He is the leading god of the Menaphite Pantheon.
textByHash.aac34ec0371f9a1d3aa6439a2f374d85660d69b4: A common saying about Tumeken is that you see him wherever you look, but never actually notice him.
textByHash.1c0ff53540a98164f6e2292c0257c5e16a4aa392: In some ways. You humans sometimes say that 'you have to be cruel to be kind'. That seems a fitting way to describe Tumeken.
textByHash.8a3dc33db7ae253dbae7a7e44b1baae5c2a272b9: Elidinis is the goddess of fertility. The River Elid is named in her honour, for it brings life to the whole region.
textByHash.53bcc12d82cb2f997d1dc226bbdd483a73d969a9: There are some who even believe the river to be Elidinis herself.
textByHash.4e1e1cac260b8834363651f2e6122ace5b7554ec: More so than many others in the pantheon, but not as much as she once was.
textByHash.2a3bba8d7bcf73ded9bb28720ea7bfb6b56f019d: Humans are fickle. Perhaps they grew bored of her?
textByHash.b3614e31b69e753c7f980b91fabad5f2562ea72e: Well if you're after the human take on things, you'd be better off asking a human.
textByHash.b53998f6d9d1c9fdd0f55f1af887727ad3cb423d: Icthlarin is the god of the dead. He is the son of Tumeken and Elidinis, and brother to the Devourer. As this is the city of the dead, he's quite popular here.
textByHash.16780d830dc032854362eeea6a5163484181bb59: Nothing of the sort. I have no interest in the gods or their followers. Instead, Icthlarin and I have an understanding.
textByHash.51cdce8e10837a975c6c70323bbbc77e16fe5df3: Cats play a significant role when it comes to guarding the dead from the Devourer. As such, they are great allies to Icthlarin.
textByHash.4998bdabf94989626289c6b487be33694b1e4998: As a thank you for looking after them, Icthlarin grants me eternal life.
textByHash.74988053b5f659fa09f2f84c3668dde24ba2159c: I'd have thought you'd be more than familiar with her by now.
textByHash.4fdf5856f07ae4b86d8f79c00156bb60deee04ba: Very well. The Devourer is the goddess of destruction. As such, she is commonly considered an evil goddess, but this is an oversimplification.
textByHash.6064ecdfe6d912fdb1a173e263c14f18537432f8: Not everything that destroys is evil. Destruction is often a necessity.
textByHash.afb0a48983b6af58c04d96dd94471bbb5cc76d15: Is causing death enough to make one evil? Icthlarin is the god of the dead. By your argument, surely he is the most evil of them all.
textByHash.a2a14eb16f6af679396fee791404d9abb8b7cabe: You clearly know little of Icthlarin and his past. Are you aware of the Mahjarrat? They once served Icthlarin, before they left him for Zamorak.
textByHash.eaba8e7992b7140087c55dc9b42d407b499011be: Or was it the Empty Lord they left him for... I forget.
textByHash.15085d50cbead7e1d6bda380123d88f44bb14d3b: Regardless, they are a race that have regularly associated themselves with gods considered evil. Interesting company for one such as Icthlarin to keep.
textByHash.10b9648e93673d533ed9fcbcc59b54dc33d29772: I never said it did. I'm just saying that things are often not as straightforward as they seem, especially when it comes to Icthlarin and the Devourer.
textByHash.0be872f8404a38435337ba37b6fabde8a07f84c3: Very well. What do you want to dicuss?
textByHash.1860aeeaf8b693b8abd828df108dc6a39983583f: Well, I suppose I do. Seeing as you're such a good guardian of cats, I'll give you one. Be careful with it, though.
textByHash.7e3f4343a13adeda873f024b912ec2444a401a48: Human, you make a mockery of my kin.
textByHash.ec17609d902451f02e76b979ffe20d53029f33e6: You should leave, before I deliver retribution for your actions.
